message:
fix(security): prevent zero-filled output on getrandom failure in crypto_shim

The previous commit introduced a bug where the result of
getrandom::fill() was ignored (let _ = ...), meaning that if the OS
CSPRNG failed, the function would silently return an array of zeros.
This amends the logic to correctly expect() success for random_bytes.

Includes a minor uncommitted assertion text update in tui_state.rs tests.
